content.full_text:
FPPC
JAN 22 1907 AH 91

FPPC
JAN 30 11:50 AM '91

RECEIVED
AND FILED
In the office of the Secretary of State
of the State of California

JAN 2 4 1991

JOHN L. HOLLIS
3600 GANADO WAY
MODESTO, CA 95356
209/521-1093

MARCH FONG EU, Secretary of State

January 17, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
P O Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attn: Technical Assistance

I am a trustee for Modesto City Schools, a very low key position
politically. I have never hired a campaign manager or treasurer.
All efforts on my campaign have been voluntary.

After the Nov. 1989 election, I terminated my committee. I do
not plan any further activity on my campaign until 1993, and then
only if I choose to run for reelection.

I missed the semi-annual campaign disclosure statement for Jan.
1, 1990 through June 30, 1990 filing date because I was not aware
ahead of time that I needed to file this form since there was no
committee or activity. My treasurer had moved out of state and I
thought all forms were complete. Stanislaus County used to send
out forms to be completed and returned. With budget constraints,
the county no longer does this. When I did get notice that this
form had to be completed, we were out of town a great deal as my
wife's father was dying of cancer. This consumed all our time
and effort. He did die in November.

Because I did not file within the ten day period, the county is
assessing me $100. They say they can not waive the penalty, only
the FPPC can do that. My appeal is that this penalty be waived
for a statement that had no activity and not even an active
committee. The statement was received by the county only three
(3) days past the grace period. I would appreciate your
consideration of this matter.

Best regards,

John L. Hollis
John L. Hollis


Due to the severe degradation and fading of this document, a reliable verbatim transcription cannot be provided. The text is too faint and unclear to accurately transcribe without significant guesswork about individual words and passages.

OFFICE OF

COUNTY CLERK-RECORDER

Telephone 209-525-6313

912-11th Street,
Room 100
Modesto, Ca. 95354

KAREN L. MATHEWS, Clerk-Recorder

September 11, 1990


Mr. John Hollis
3600 Ganado Way
Modesto CA 95356

RE: FPPC Campaign Disclosure Penalty

Dear Mr. Hollis,

According to our records, your campaign disclosure statement was
due on July 31, 1990. The statement was not filed until
September 7, 1990, despite our letter dated August 22, 1990 urging
that you file within 10 days.

Government Code Section 91013 imposes a $10 a day fine after the
filing deadline until the statement is filed. The liability is limited
to the cumulative amount of the contributions or expenditures for
the period covered by the late statement or $100, whichever is
greater. Furthermore, the filing officer is prohibited by law from
waiving any portion of the liability if the statement is not filed
within 10 days after the filing officer sends you specific written
notice of the filing requirements.

Therefore, please remit to the Stanislaus County Clerk at 912-
11th Street, Room 100, Modesto, CA 95354, your check or money
order in the amount of $100.

Additionally, the form filed (Short Form) is inappropriate. Any
officeholder who has a committee must use a Form 490 (Long Form)
until the committee is terminated. Please amend your report using
the attached forms.

Sincerely,


Karen L. Mathews
County Clerk-Recorder

Encl [illegible] Jul, 1990 so
KLM/nch short form was OK, ench

[illegible] rd 90 to Robert Hamlin, Treasurer, 817 Daffet Blvd, Eminence [illegible]
